text:
The wise owl once told me that there's a secret garden just beyond the forest's edge where butterflies flit and flowers bloom seven days a week. I believe it. The first time I wandered there, the sun was just sinking low in the sky. There was a small clearing, hidden away amongst ancient oaks, its center surrounded by a bed-of-roses I'd never seen. Every petal swayed in harmony with the breeze. But the most enchanting part? A cluster of tiny, bright blue butterflies flitting about. They fluttered toward me.

One landed on a buttonhook near my feet. I scooped it up, smiled, and held it up. Its eyes fluttered invitingly. After a long pause, it nestled in my hand and fluttered closed. I stroked its wings. We sat there for hours, until the moon rose and the stars filled the night sky.

Every visit has been like that. Each time I stand in that garden, butterflies
